title: Find perimeter by counting units
description: Calculate the perimeter of a rectangle from its side lengths.
topic_path: Get ready for area and perimeter

assessment_items.question_content:
Each square on the grid is $1$ unit square.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the perimeter of the shaded rectangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

[[☃ image 1]]



To find the perimeter, we add the lengths of all $4$ sides.

$3+6+3+6= \text{ ?}$
The perimeter is $18$ units.

options.alt: A rectangle with 3 rows and 6 columns on a grid. There are 18 unit squares in the rectangle.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each square on the grid is $1$ unit square.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the perimeter of the shaded rectangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

[[☃ image 1]]



To find the perimeter, we add the lengths of all $4$ sides.

$8+9+8+9= \text{ ?}$
The perimeter is $34$ units.
